channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: FG halts WAEC/NECO fee increase for further review
item.description: The Federal Government has paused its plan to raise registration fees for the 2027 West African Senior School Certificate Examination (WASSCE) and National Examinations Council (NECO) Senior School Ce...

item.title: Former Foreign Office official challenges prime minister's decision to dismiss him over security clearance dispute
item.description: Sir Olly Robbins, a senior British civil servant, has launched a judicial review against Prime Minister Keir Starmer after being sacked for failing to disclose that Lord Peter Mandelson had not passed...

item.title: Brisbane restaurants offer steak deals amid rising prices
item.description: Brisbane's high-end dining scene is adapting to inflationary pressures by offering steak frites deals, with several restaurants providing affordable all-you-can-eat options. Monal Dining, Petite, Augu...

item.title: President William Ruto Commends Migori Youth FC's Promotion and Announces Sports Infrastructure Plans
item.description: President William Ruto hosted Migori Youth FC at State House after the club secured promotion to the Kenyan Premier League. During the event, Ruto humorously inquired why guests hadn't been served tea...

item.title: William Ruto Maintains Lead in 2027 Kenyan Presidential Race Amid Mixed Support
item.description: A new Infotrak survey released on July 13, 2026, reveals President William Ruto holds a commanding lead in Kenya's 2027 presidential race with 32% support, more than double his nearest challenger, Wip...

item.title: TSC Online Services in Kenya: A 2026 Guide for Teachers
item.description: The article explains how Kenyan teachers can access essential services through the Teachers Service Commission (TSC) online portal. Key services include viewing payslips, applying for transfers, wealt...

item.title: IED Attack in KP's Tank Leaves 2 Cops Martyred and Several Injured
item.description: A suspected improvised explosive device (IED) attack targeted a police armoured personnel carrier (APC) on Tank-Jandola Road in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa’s Tank district, resulting in the martyrdom of two of...

item.title: 6.4 kg hydroponic weed seized at Delhi Airport, three arrests made
item.description: Customs officers at Delhi's Indira Gandhi International Airport arrested three passengers after seizing 6.37 kg of suspected hydroponic weed concealed in aircraft lavatories, under seats, and in bagga...

item.title: Remi Tinubu urges wealthy Nigerians and top entertainers to expand charitable support for vulnerable citizens
item.description: Nigeria's First Lady, Senator Oluremi Tinubu, has appealed to some of the country's biggest music stars, including Davido, Burna Boy and Asake, as well as other wealthy Nigerians, to invest more in ch...

item.title: Ombeni Sefue advocates for peaceful political expression and cautions against adopting foreign protest methods
item.description: Former Chief Secretary and diplomat Ambassador Ombeni Sefue emphasized the importance of peaceful political expression in Tanzania, warning against copying violent protest cultures from abroad. In an ...

item.title: Sam Neill, Iconic Actor of Jurassic Park and The Piano, Dies at 78
item.description: The New Zealand actor Sam Neill, renowned for his iconic roles in films such as Jurassic Park, Peaky Blinders, and The Piano, has passed away at the age of 78. His career spanned over 150 film and tel...

item.title: Sindh High Court Acquits Man in 2017 University Student Suicide Case After Legal Review
item.description: The Sindh High Court (SHC) acquitted Anees Khaskheli, a private school teacher, in the 2017 suicide case of Naila Rind, a final-year Sindhi student whose body was found hanging in her hostel room. Ini...

item.title: Stock Market Volatility Expected as Investors Navigate Uncertain Markets
item.description: The stock market is poised for a volatile trading session as investors grapple with geopolitical tensions, inflationary pressures, and mixed economic data. Major indices like the Dow Jones Industrial ...

item.title: Kerala Labour Laws and Retrenchment Rules: Government to Address Legal Concerns
item.description: The article discusses the recent retrenchment of approximately 800 employees by the US-based tech firm CorroHealth Infotech Private Limited in Kerala, sparking legal debates. The company cited operati...

item.title: Inquest opened into death of Bafana midfielder Jayden Adams
item.description: Cape Town police have initiated an inquest following the tragic death of 25-year-old Bafana Bafana and Mamelodi Sundowns midfielder Jayden Adams, whose body was discovered at a property in Schotsche K...

item.title: Canberra's Riley Hamilton earns AFL Rising Star nomination after standout debut
item.description: Riley Hamilton, a 19-year-old rookie with the GWS Giants, has earned an AFL Rising Star nomination following a standout performance in his second game. The Canberra product made his AFL debut in an up...

item.title: Sam Neill: A Versatile Actor's Legacy in Cinema
item.description: Sam Neill, the acclaimed New Zealand-born actor, passed away at 76, leaving behind a legacy of iconic roles and contributions to cinema. Renowned for his versatility, Neill's career spanned over five ...

item.title: HDFC Bank Reduces Staff Amid Automation Push to Boost Efficiency
item.description: HDFC Bank, India's largest private lender, has reduced its workforce by 3,343 employees in the last financial year as part of an aggressive automation initiative. The bank's total staff decreased to 2...

item.title: Pastor Kanyari Links Kenyans to Gulf Job Opportunities
item.description: Prophet Victor Kanyari, a controversial Kenyan preacher, announced job opportunities in Gulf countries like Dubai, Kuwait, Bahrain, Abu Dhabi, Saudi Arabia, and Oman during a Sunday church service. He...

item.title: Nigerian Students Shouldn't Bear WAEC/NECO Fees – Aisha Yesufu Advocates Free Education
item.description: Aisha Yesufu, a prominent Nigerian activist, has strongly opposed the proposed ₦50,000 fee hike for WAEC and NECO exams, calling it unjust. The Federal Government initially approved the increase, effe...
